concerns.text: T010 and T011 (Contract tests) are listed in Phase 2 but explicitly depend on T006 (Schema definition). However, T006 is marked [P] (parallel-safe) alongside T004, T005, T008, T009. If T006 runs in parallel with T010/T011, the tests will fail due to missing artifacts. T010/T011 must be moved to Phase 3 or explicitly sequenced after T006, breaking the 'Foundational' parallel block.

T016 (Straight-lining detector) depends on T013 (Stimuli) and T014 (Ratings). T014 depends on T009 (Power Analysis). The task list places T016 in Phase 3, but T014 is also in Phase 3. If T016 runs before T014 completes, it fails. The 'Checkpoint' implies T016 is ready, but the dependency chain T009 -> T014 -> T016 is not strictly enforced by phase separation if T014 is not marked as a hard prerequisite for T016 within the phase.

T028 (Sensitivity engine) depends on T025 (Result serialization). T025 is listed as an implementation task in Phase 4 but is marked [X] (unchecked) in the provided text, while T020-T024 are [X]. If T025 is not completed before T028 runs, the sensitivity analysis fails. T025 must be explicitly marked as a prerequisite for T028, or T025 must be moved to Phase 4 completion before Phase 5 begins.

T009 (Power Analysis) is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, T009 produces `data/processed/power_analysis_results.json` which is a hard input for T014 (Simulation). If T009 runs in parallel with other Phase 2 tasks, it is safe, but if T014 (Phase 3) is started before T009 finishes, it fails. The 'Parallel Example' section suggests T009 can run in parallel, but the dependency on T014 is not visually enforced in the phase structure, risking a race condition if the pipeline is automated.

T012 (Unit test for straight-lining) is listed in Phase 2 (Foundational) but tests logic implemented in T016 (Phase 3). This violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule for tests; the test cannot exist before the logic it tests is implemented. T012 should be moved to Phase 3, after T016.
